pattern: I + am / You/We/They + are / He/She/It + is
correct_example: She is a teacher.

common_errors.wrong: She are a teacher.
She am a teacher.

key_signal: 主語の人称と数でbe動詞を選ぶ
sources: Purdue OWL: Making Subjects and Verbs Agree

pattern: He/She/It + V-s / I/You/We/They + V(原形)
correct_example: He plays tennis every day.

common_errors.wrong: He play tennis every day.
common_errors.why: 三人称単数にはsが必要

common_errors.wrong: They plays tennis.

key_signal: 主語が三人称単数かどうか

pattern: There is + 単数名詞 / There are + 複数名詞
correct_example: There are many books on the shelf.

common_errors.wrong: There is many books.
There have many books.

key_signal: 後続の名詞が単数か複数か
sources: Cambridge Grammar: Nouns, pronouns and determiners

pattern: Do/Does + S + V(原形) ...?
correct_example: Does she like coffee?

common_errors.wrong: Does she likes coffee?
Is she like coffee?
common_errors.why: 一般動詞の疑問文にbe動詞は使わない

key_signal: 一般動詞 → Do/Does、be動詞 → Is/Are

pattern: S + V-ed(規則) / S + V(不規則変化) + 過去の時間表現
correct_example: I had breakfast this morning.

common_errors.wrong: I have breakfast this morning.
I haved breakfast.

key_signal: yesterday, last ~, ago, this morning → 過去形

pattern: S + will + V(原形)
correct_example: I will call you tomorrow.

common_errors.wrong: I will calls you tomorrow.
I will to call you.

key_signal: tomorrow, next ~, soon → will

pattern: S + am/is/are + V-ing
correct_example: The children are playing in the garden now.

common_errors.wrong: The children playing now.
The children is playing.

key_signal: now, right now, at the moment → 進行形

rule: 不定詞の形容詞的用法
pattern: something/nothing/anything + to + V(原形)
correct_example: I want something to drink.

common_errors.wrong: I want something for drink.
I want something drinking.

key_signal: -thing/-one/-where + to + 動詞原形

rule: 句動詞 look up / look for / look at
pattern: look up = 調べる / look for = 探す / look at = 見る
correct_example: Please look up this word in the dictionary.

common_errors.wrong: Please look for this word in the dictionary.
Please look this word.

key_signal: 動詞+前置詞/副詞の組み合わせで意味が変わる

pattern: be good/bad/interested/surprised + at/in/by + N/-ing
correct_example: He is good at math.

common_errors.wrong: He is good in math.
He is good for math.

key_signal: 形容詞+前置詞の組み合わせは固定

pattern: S + have/has + pp + since/for ...
correct_example: She has lived in Tokyo since 2020.

common_errors.wrong: She lived in Tokyo since 2020.
common_errors.why: sinceは現在完了のシグナル。過去形は不可

common_errors.wrong: She is living in Tokyo since 2020.
common_errors.why: sinceと進行形の組み合わせは不自然

key_signal: since + 時点 / for + 期間 → 現在完了

pattern: S + have/has + (ever/never) + pp
correct_example: I have never been to Kyoto.

common_errors.wrong: I never went to Kyoto.
common_errors.why: 経験の否定はhave never + pp

common_errors.wrong: I have never went to Kyoto.

key_signal: ever/never/before/once/twice → 現在完了（経験）

pattern: S + had + pp (過去のある時点より前の出来事)
correct_example: When I arrived, the movie had already started.

common_errors.wrong: When I arrived, the movie already started.
When I arrived, the movie has started.
common_errors.why: 過去の文脈でhas（現在完了）は不可

key_signal: 過去の文中で「さらに前の出来事」→ had + pp

pattern: S + be動詞 + pp (+ by 動作主)
correct_example: This cake was made by my mother.

common_errors.wrong: This cake was make by my mother.
common_errors.why: be + 過去分詞。makeではなくmade

common_errors.wrong: This cake made by my mother.

key_signal: 「～される」→ be + 過去分詞

pattern: good/well → better → best / bad/badly → worse → worst
correct_example: He speaks English better than his brother.

common_errors.wrong: He speaks English more good than his brother.
common_errors.why: goodの比較級はbetter。more goodは不可

common_errors.wrong: He speaks English gooder.
common_errors.why: 不規則変化。-erは付かない

key_signal: good/well/bad/far/many/much は不規則変化
sources: Purdue OWL: How to Use Adjectives and Adverbs

pattern: 先行詞(人) + who + V ...
correct_example: The man who is standing over there is my uncle.

common_errors.wrong: The man which is standing...
The man what is standing...
common_errors.why: whatは関係代名詞として先行詞を取れない

key_signal: 人→who/whom/whose、物→which/that

rule: 時・条件の副詞節では現在形
pattern: If/When/Before/After/Until + S + V(現在形), S + will + V
correct_example: If it rains tomorrow, we will stay home.

common_errors.wrong: If it will rain tomorrow, we will stay home.
If it rained tomorrow, we will stay home.
common_errors.why: 単なる条件なら現在形。仮定法は別

key_signal: if/when/before/after/until の中は現在形（未来でも）

rule: 使役動詞 make + O + 原形
pattern: make + O + V(原形不定詞) ※toは不要
correct_example: He made me write the report again.

common_errors.wrong: He made me to write the report.
He made me writing the report.

key_signal: make/let/have + O + 原形（toなし）

pattern: look forward to + V-ing (toは前置詞)
correct_example: I'm looking forward to seeing you again.

common_errors.wrong: I'm looking forward to see you.
common_errors.why: toは前置詞→後ろは動名詞(-ing)

common_errors.wrong: I'm looking forward seeing you.

key_signal: to + -ing になる表現: look forward to, be used to, object to

pattern: Neither A nor B + V(Bに一致)
correct_example: Neither Tom nor Jerry was at the party.

common_errors.wrong: Neither Tom or Jerry was at the party.
Neither Tom nor Jerry were at the party.

key_signal: neither...nor / either...or / not only...but also
sources: Purdue OWL: Making Subjects and Verbs Agree

rule: 不可算名詞は単数扱い
pattern: news/information/advice/furniture + is (不可算→単数)
correct_example: The news was very surprising.

common_errors.wrong: The news were very surprising.
common_errors.why: newsは不可算名詞→単数扱いwas

common_errors.wrong: The informations are useful.

key_signal: news, information, advice, furniture, luggage → 不可算
sources: Purdue OWL: Count and Noncount Nouns

pattern: whether + S + V + or not = ～かどうか
correct_example: I don't know whether he will come or not.

common_errors.wrong: I don't know what he will come or not.
I don't know that he will come or not.

key_signal: ～かどうか → whether (or not) / if
sources: Cambridge Grammar: Words, sentences and clauses

pattern: do + 人 + a favor = 頼みを聞く
correct_example: Could you do me a favor?

common_errors.wrong: Could you make me a favor?
Could you give me a favor?

key_signal: 定型: do a favor, make a decision, take a break

pattern: S + will have + pp + by + 未来の時点
correct_example: By the time you arrive, I will have finished the work.

common_errors.wrong: By the time you arrive, I will finish the work.
By the time you will arrive, I will have finished.

key_signal: by + 未来の期限 → will have + pp

pattern: S + had been + V-ing + for/since ... (過去のある時点まで継続)
correct_example: She had been studying for three hours when I got home.

common_errors.wrong: She has been studying for three hours when I got home.
She was studying for three hours when I got home.
common_errors.why: forで期間を示す→完了進行形

key_signal: 過去の時点まで動作が継続 → had been + -ing

pattern: The ___ of ... → 名詞が必要（introduce→introduction, decide→decision）
correct_example: The introduction of the new policy was announced yesterday.

common_errors.wrong: The introduce of the new policy...
common_errors.why: 冠詞Theの後は名詞。動詞は不可

common_errors.wrong: The introducing of the new policy...
common_errors.why: 動名詞よりも派生名詞が自然

key_signal: 冠詞(The/A) + ___ + of → 名詞形が必要

pattern: need/want/expect + to be + pp
correct_example: The report needs to be submitted by Friday.

common_errors.wrong: The report needs to submit by Friday.
common_errors.why: reportは提出される側→to be submitted

common_errors.wrong: The report needs submitted.

key_signal: 主語が動作を受ける側 → to be + pp

pattern: due to + 名詞句 / because + S + V(節)
correct_example: The meeting was postponed due to the heavy rain.

common_errors.wrong: The meeting was postponed because the heavy rain.
The meeting was postponed due to it rained.

key_signal: due to / owing to + 名詞 vs because / since + 節

pattern: refrain from + V-ing = ～を控える
correct_example: Please refrain from using your phone during the meeting.

common_errors.wrong: Please refrain to use your phone.
Please refrain using your phone.

key_signal: refrain from / prevent from / prohibit from + -ing

pattern: 先行詞(場所) + where + S + V (= in/at which)
correct_example: The city where I was born is very small.

common_errors.wrong: The city which I was born is very small.
common_errors.why: whichならin whichが必要。whereで代替

common_errors.wrong: The city what I was born.

key_signal: 場所→where、時→when、理由→why

pattern: However + 形容詞/副詞 + S + V = どんなに～しても
correct_example: However hard he tried, he couldn't solve the problem.

common_errors.wrong: Whatever hard he tried...
common_errors.why: 形容詞/副詞を修飾→however。whateverは名詞

common_errors.wrong: However he tried hard...
common_errors.why: howeverの直後に形容詞/副詞を置く

key_signal: however + adj/adv / whatever + noun / whenever + time
sources: Cambridge Grammar: Words, sentences and clauses

rule: 仮定法現在 suggest/recommend + 原形
pattern: suggest/recommend/insist/demand + that + S + V(原形)
correct_example: I suggest that he attend the meeting.

common_errors.wrong: I suggest that he attends the meeting.
common_errors.why: 仮定法現在→三単現のsは付かない

common_errors.wrong: I suggest that he should attends.

key_signal: suggest/recommend/insist/demand + that S + 原形

rule: The number of（単数）vs A number of（複数）
rule_en: The Number Of vs A Number Of
pattern: The number of + 複数名詞 + 単数動詞 / A number of + 複数名詞 + 複数動詞
correct_example: The number of students is increasing every year.

common_errors.wrong: The number of students are increasing.
common_errors.why: The number of = ～の数（単数扱い）

common_errors.wrong: A number of students is studying.
common_errors.why: A number of = 多くの（複数扱い）

key_signal: The number of → is / A number of → are
sources: Purdue OWL: Making Subjects and Verbs Agree

pattern: 物・事 → -ing(boring/exciting) / 人の感情 → -ed(bored/excited)
correct_example: The lecture was boring. / I was bored.

common_errors.wrong: The lecture was bored.
I was boring.

key_signal: 原因（物）→ -ing / 感じる側（人）→ -ed

pattern: succeed in + V-ing = ～に成功する
correct_example: He succeeded in passing the exam.

common_errors.wrong: He succeeded to pass the exam.
He succeeded passing the exam.

key_signal: succeed in / consist of / insist on / depend on + -ing

pattern: If + S + had + pp, S + would have + pp
correct_example: If I had known about the meeting, I would have attended.

common_errors.wrong: If I knew about the meeting, I would have attended.
common_errors.why: 過去の事実に反する→had known

common_errors.wrong: If I had known, I would attend.

key_signal: 過去の事実に反する仮定 → If had pp, would have pp

pattern: pp + 前置詞句, S + V (主語が動作を受ける側)
correct_example: Seen from the top of the mountain, the city looked beautiful.

common_errors.wrong: Seeing from the top, the city looked beautiful.
See from the top, the city looked beautiful.
common_errors.why: 分詞構文は分詞で始まる

key_signal: 主語が受動→過去分詞で開始、能動→現在分詞で開始

pattern: Not only + 倒置(助動詞+S) + but (also) + S + V
correct_example: Not only does he speak English, but he also speaks French.

common_errors.wrong: Not only he speaks English...
common_errors.why: Not onlyが文頭→倒置が必要

common_errors.wrong: Not only does he speaks...

key_signal: 否定語句が文頭→倒置: Not only / Never / Hardly / Seldom
sources: Cambridge Grammar: Words, sentences and clauses

pattern: insist on + V-ing = ～することを主張する
correct_example: The manager insisted on seeing the documents himself.

common_errors.wrong: The manager insisted to see the documents.
The manager insisted seeing the documents.

key_signal: insist on / persist in / count on + -ing

pattern: would rather + S + V(過去形) = Sに～してほしい（仮定法）
correct_example: I would rather you didn't smoke in this room.

common_errors.wrong: I would rather you don't smoke.
I would rather you not smoke.
common_errors.why: S + Vの構文ではdidn'tが必要

key_signal: would rather + S + 過去形（仮定法的表現）

rule: It is (high) time + S + 過去形
pattern: It is (high/about) time + S + V(過去形)
correct_example: It is high time we made a decision.

common_errors.wrong: It is high time we make a decision.
It is high time we will make a decision.

key_signal: It's time + 過去形 = そろそろ～すべき

rule: 仮定法過去完了の倒置
rule_en: Inversion in Third Conditional (Had + S + pp)
pattern: Had + S + pp, S + would have + pp (= If S had pp)
correct_example: Had I known the truth, I would have acted differently.

common_errors.wrong: Had I known the truth, I would act differently.
Had I know the truth...
common_errors.why: had + 過去分詞。原形は不可

key_signal: If省略→倒置: Had S pp / Were S to / Should S

pattern: come into effect/force = 施行される、発効する
correct_example: The new regulation will come into effect next month.

common_errors.wrong: The new regulation will come in effect.
The new regulation will come to effect.

key_signal: come into: effect/force/being/existence/contact

pattern: Hardly + had + S + pp + when + S + V(過去形)
correct_example: Hardly had he begun the presentation when the fire alarm went off.

common_errors.wrong: Hardly he had begun...
Hardly had he begun... than the alarm.

key_signal: Hardly/Scarcely + had S pp + when / No sooner + had S pp + than
sources: Cambridge Grammar: Words, sentences and clauses

rule: Were it not for（仮定法倒置）
pattern: Were it not for + N, S + would/could + V = もし～がなければ
correct_example: Were it not for his help, we could not have succeeded.

common_errors.wrong: Were it not his help...
common_errors.why: forが必要。Were it not for + 名詞

common_errors.wrong: Was it not for his help...

key_signal: Were it not for = If it were not for = But for
